chunk_summary: Guidelines for fortification escalation and de-escalation in NICU settings, focusing on nutrition targets, growth triggers, and biochemical markers for preterm infants receiving expressed or donor milk.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise approach to fortification escalation and de-escalation in preterm infants.
Learn to identify growth and biochemical triggers for adjusting fortification levels.
Recognize when to reduce or stop fortification as infants approach term-equivalent maturity.
key_facts: Step 0: Verify daily intake (mL/kg/day) and correct for losses from spillage or unrecorded residuals.
Step 1: Initiate multi-nutrient fortifier at 80–100 mL/kg/day (half-strength for 24–48 hours if tolerance is uncertain).
Step 2: Increase protein delivery if weight gain is <15 g/kg/day for 3 consecutive days despite full-strength fortifier.
Step 3: Low BUN with poor growth supports increasing protein; high BUN requires assessing hydration and renal function.
De-escalation: Reduce fortification as infants approach term maturity, with a step-down to half-strength for 48–72 hours before discontinuation.
reference_quote: Fortification of human milk for preterm infants: update and recommendations of the European Milk Bank Association Working Group — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm...
document_key_topics: Growth and biochemical triggers for fortification adjustments
De-escalation strategies for fortification in NICU
